title: US Privacy and Data Protection Notice
content:
Meridian Wealth Partners Privacy and Data Protection Notice

Effective Date: January 1, 2025
Last Updated: February 15, 2025

Meridian Wealth Partners ("MWP," "we," "us," or "our") is committed to protecting the privacy and confidentiality of our clients' personal and financial information. As a registered investment adviser under the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 and subject to SEC oversight, we maintain comprehensive privacy safeguards in accordance with SEC Regulation S-P and the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act (GLBA).

Information We Collect

We collect nonpublic personal information about you from various sources to provide comprehensive wealth management services. This includes information from account applications and forms (such as name, address, Social Security number, assets, and income), transaction history and account balances, credit reports and financial statements, and information from conversations with our advisory team. For high-net-worth clients with portfolios exceeding $5 million, we may also collect enhanced due diligence information including source of wealth documentation and beneficial ownership details.

How We Use Your Information

Your personal information enables us to provide tailored investment advisory services, including portfolio management, financial planning, and estate planning coordination. We analyze your financial data to construct appropriate asset allocation models, typically ranging from conservative portfolios with 30% equity exposure to aggressive growth portfolios with up to 85% equity allocation. Our proprietary risk assessment algorithms utilize your demographic and financial information to determine suitable investment strategies within your specified risk tolerance parameters.

Information Sharing Practices

Under SEC Regulation S-P, we are required to provide you with our privacy policies and practices regarding client information. We do not sell, rent, or otherwise distribute your personal information to unaffiliated third parties for marketing purposes. However, we may share information with service providers essential to account administration, including our qualified custodian, portfolio management systems, and compliance monitoring services.

We may disclose your information to unaffiliated third parties as permitted by law, including: responses to subpoenas or court orders, regulatory examinations by the SEC or state authorities, fraud prevention and legal compliance purposes, and with your explicit written consent. When working with institutional clients or family offices managing assets exceeding $25 million, additional reporting requirements may necessitate enhanced information sharing protocols.

Safeguarding Measures

Our cybersecurity framework employs multiple layers of protection, including 256-bit SSL encryption for all client communications, multi-factor authentication requirements for account access, annual penetration testing by certified third-party security firms, and segregated network architecture isolating client data systems. All MWP associates undergo mandatory annual privacy training and are subject to strict confidentiality agreements.

We maintain physical safeguards including locked filing systems, restricted facility access, and secure document destruction procedures. Electronic safeguards include firewall protection, intrusion detection systems, and encrypted data transmission protocols meeting institutional banking standards.

Your Privacy Rights

You have the right to opt out of certain information sharing arrangements, request copies of information we maintain about you, and receive notification of any material changes to our privacy practices. California residents may have additional rights under the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA).

For questions regarding this Privacy Notice or to exercise your privacy rights, please contact our Chief Compliance Officer at compliance@meridianwealth.com or (555) 123-4567. This notice applies to current and former clients, and we reserve the right to modify these policies with appropriate advance notice as required under federal regulations.
